overview: The Village of Weston Community Life and Public Safety Committee met on May 4th, approving amendments to the nuisance ordinance (Chapter 50), recommending an e-bike ordinance with restrictions on emotos, and authorizing a raze order for a deteriorated structure at 4104 Shore Avenue. The committee also discussed overnight parking regulations and a potential crosswalk near Mount Olive Church, deferring both items for further research.
committee: Community Life and Public Safety Committee

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Approval of minutes from previous meeting
Acknowledgment of department reports (Code Enforcement, Fire/EMS, Police)
Mobile food vendors fire inspection ordinance

discussions.body: Jim Senalt spoke extensively about concerns with the new farmers market location at Kennedy Park, predicting grass damage from vehicle parking. He questioned whether parking on grass violates village code and raised ADA accessibility concerns about handicap spaces being on grass rather than asphalt. He also questioned the need for new fire inspections on food trucks and criticized the age of some code enforcement cases, noting some have been pending for years without visible progress.

Code Enforcement reported that 3020 Mountain View Avenue will be removed from the enforcement list after the property sale was completed and the new owner submitted repair permits. Chief Hunt reported the police department had 2,394 calls in March (77 per day). The department has hired its 40th officer, with the 38th starting last week, 39th starting in May, and 40th around January. Two SROs work on zoning violations during summer for Weston, Rothschild, and Schofield. Motion to acknowledge reports 5, 6, and 7 passed unanimously.

The committee reviewed final amendments to the nuisance ordinance, including previously discussed changes to tree limb heights over streets and walkways (14 and 10 feet) and compliance with jurisdictional authority requirements. Motion by Olsen, seconded by Campkey, to recommend approval to the Board of Trustees passed unanimously.

Extensive discussion occurred regarding the proposed e-bike ordinance. Chief Hunt strongly advocated for prohibiting emotos (electric dirt bikes) from roadways and sidewalks, comparing them to unlicensed dirt bikes. Committee members discussed whether electric scooters used by children should be restricted, with Chief Hunt suggesting officer discretion for 'kids play scooters.' The committee noted Rothschild was considering the ordinance, Wausau was making adjustments, and Schofield declined. Motion by Olsen, seconded by Sherwanka, to recommend approval with the addition that emotos are prohibited from roadways, sidewalks, trails, and public property passed unanimously.

Trustee Tolson presented research showing Weston's 12-hour parking restriction (4 AM to 4 PM, October 31-May 1) is significantly longer than surrounding municipalities, which typically have 4-5 hour windows. Michael Wodalski from Public Works noted the even/odd system was implemented because plowing typically doesn't start until 4 AM and residential streets aren't reached until 7-8 AM. Chief Hunt preferred keeping overnight restrictions to give officers time to clear vehicles before plowing. The committee decided to defer the item, asking Wodalski to identify 6-8 specific problem areas for targeted signage rather than village-wide restrictions.

Mount Olive Church requested a crosswalk to connect their facility with DC Everest school parking they use during services. Staff estimated a basic crosswalk would cost approximately $2,000, while a lighted system would be $25,000-$30,000. Committee members expressed reluctance to set a precedent of paying for requested crosswalks. Motion by Olsen, seconded by Sherwanka, that if Mount Olive agrees to cover the installation cost, the committee recommends board approval. Motion passed unanimously.

Chief Hunt explained a regional fire inspection program involving Wisconsin Rapids, Marshfield, Stevens Point, Plover, Rib Mountain, and Weston Safer departments. Food trucks inspected by any participating department receive a sticker valid across all jurisdictions, eliminating redundant inspections. Wausau opted out to maintain their own system. Chief Hunt noted a recent inspection failed a vendor due to improper galvanized steel LP piping and unsecured propane cylinders. Motion by Sherwanka, seconded by Campkey, to approve Ordinance 26-8 passed unanimously.

Staff presented three address corrections needed for E911 compliance: 5915 Delicoski Street (duplex needing separate addresses), Ross Avenue addresses out of sequence near an apartment building, and 2211 Radkey Avenue for a new duplex on a private drive. Chief Hunt added there's another issue on Delicoski at Weston Villa Apartments where an 8-plex has 9 separate addresses including a utility room. Motion passed unanimously to recommend corrections to the Board.

Staff requested authorization to begin raze proceedings for an abandoned structure at 4104 Shore Avenue. The property has been abandoned since 2010-2011 and was deemed too deteriorated for a fire department training burn. Staff discovered chickens were being housed inside the structure after receiving reports of lights on in the building. The current owners, who are current on taxes, prefer the village proceed with demolition. Costs will be recovered through the tax roll. Motion by Olsen, seconded by Campkey, passed unanimously.

publicComment: Jim Senalt provided extensive public comment criticizing the new farmers market location at Kennedy Park, raising concerns about grass damage from parking, ADA accessibility issues with handicap spaces on grass, questions about the origin of new food truck fire inspection requirements, and lack of progress on long-standing code enforcement cases. He stated some cases are 'almost old enough to vote' and requested the committee receive progress updates on older cases.
actionItems: Chapter 50 nuisance amendments recommended to Board of Trustees for approval
E-bike ordinance with emoto prohibition on roadways, sidewalks, trails, and public property recommended to Board of Trustees
Michael Wodalski to identify 6-8 specific problem areas for targeted overnight parking signage and return to committee in July
Michael Wodalski to contact Mount Olive Church regarding cost-sharing for crosswalk installation
Ordinance 26-8 for mobile food vendor fire inspections approved
Address corrections for Delicoski Street, Ross Avenue, and Radkey Avenue recommended to Board
Raze order for 4104 Shore Avenue authorized
Staff to investigate property condition at Sternberg and South Timber
Next meeting scheduled for Monday, July 7th at 5:00 PM
title: Community Life and Public Safety
